finding_results.notes: Deck exchange: Replaced three bare prompts with fuller turns. Line 23 'How.' expanded to 'How'd she bring it up.' breaking consecutive bare-prompt clustering with line 27. Line 39 'Why not.' expanded to name explicit scene pressure (homicide + safe awareness). Line 63 'Paul.' expanded to 'Paul. Just say it.' adding interpersonal pressure. Result: zero consecutive one-or-two-word prompt lines from Joel; at least four challenge turns name explicit scene pressure (lines 31, 35, 39, 43). Study argument: Line 421 Paul's speech gains a restart/self-correction mid-list ('I'm not talking about hiding the—' He stopped), showing sleep-deprived cornered speech rather than rhetorically settled negation. Line 455 Joel's 'one hour' condition gains unfinished phrasing ('if you come back telling me he cried and that changes the—' He stopped himself), restoring alive spoken texture from the pre-revision snapshot. Line 459 Paul's callback revised from procedural ('lead with the paper either') to the more alive 'lead with cried.' Contraction fix: Line 307 Paul's 'We are out over our skis' contracted to 'We're' consistent with moderate contraction level under stress.

finding_results.notes: Reworked the flagged emphasis cluster so the beats no longer stack the same denial-then-correction scaffold. The RT recognition now lands through immediacy, Martin/Tommy recognition through image, the fire-document voice through descriptive contrast, the Ray Toomey beat through scene image, the assembled-case turn through structural metaphor, and Joel's caution through his analytic reflex.

pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 16 now lands the RT document as an embodied revelation, makes the one-hour delay a knowingly worse but character-true choice with clear document custody, restores Paul and Joel's pressured spoken register, and resolves the August 2002 fire-date continuity mismatch.

Six targeted edits address the single finding. In the deck exchange, three of Joel's bare one-word prompts were expanded into fuller turns carrying scene pressure (Mallick's approach, the homicide context, Paul's hesitation), eliminating all consecutive bare-prompt clusters while preserving the cross-examination rhythm that is character-true for Joel. In the study argument, Paul's climactic speech and Joel's 'one hour' condition both gain live-speech roughness — a mid-sentence restart and an aborted sarcastic threat — replacing composed thesis-language with the friction of two exhausted men negotiating under time pressure. One contraction fix aligns Paul's dialogue with his moderate contraction level under stress. All edits are local to the cited defect zones; chapter architecture, must-land beats, the fire-document payoff, and the boundary handoff to Chapter 17 are unchanged.
Addressed the packet's prose finding by varying six repeated negation-turn beats in chapter 16 while preserving the chapter's deduction engine, continuity facts, and Joel/Paul voice signatures.
